etymology.description: phonetic 來 (láí, 'come') + semantic 夊 (suī, 'footprint pointing down; to walk slowly') -> wheat
etymology.interpretation: 麥(麥) (mài, 'wheat') uses 來(lái, 'come') as a phonetic component, reflecting the sound. The semantic component 夊 (suī, 'footprint pointing down; to walk slowly'), signifies the growth pattern of wheat as a plant steadily growing upward. Historically, 來 (lái, 'come') was used for 'wheat', but as it was borrowed for the meaning 'to come', the new character 麥 was created to distinctly represent 'wheat'.
etymology.simplification: The traditional character 麥 was simplified to 麦 by removing the lower part 夊 (suī, 'footprint pointing down; to walk slowly') and retaining a simplified upper structure that captures the phonetic essence rather than the complete semantic picture.

parts.english: footprint pointing down; to walk slowly

examples.chinese: 田地里种满了麦子(田地裡種滿了麥子)。
examples.english: The fields are full of wheat.

examples.chinese: 麦芽糖是用麦子制作的(麥芽糖是用麥子製作的)。
examples.pinyin: Màiyátáng shì yòng màizi zhìzuò de.
examples.english: Malt sugar is made from wheat.

examples.chinese: 小麦面粉可以用来做面包(小麥麵粉可以用來做麵包)。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎomài miànfěn kěyǐ yòng lái zuò miànbāo.
examples.english: Wheat flour can be used to make bread.
